danger_signals.visual_form: Polygonal 3D enemy ships with aggressive angular designs, distinct from player's Arwing silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile threat requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD shield strength meter decreasing, visual damage indicators on player craft
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage taken; craft vulnerability increasing

danger_signals.visual_form: Wingman aircraft entering player view under fire or being chased by enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Ally in immediate danger; player intervention required to prevent ally loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale enemy or creature appearing at level end, distinctive from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Critical progression gate; defeat required to advance

danger_signals.visual_form: Terrain features, obstacles, and structures that can damage the Arwing (wings clipping)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; weapon upgrade loss and handling degradation if struck
Reduced offensive capability; need to seek power-ups

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct visual objects floating in environment, graphically differentiated from enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Restorative or enhancement opportunity (shield recovery, ammunition, weapon upgrade)

safety_signals.visual_form: Three ally aircraft visible during non-boss gameplay, traveling alongside player
safety_signals.meaning: Active support available; allies can engage enemies and provide tactical assistance

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible corridor or space free from immediate enemy fire or obstacles
HUD boost meter filled or available for use
safety_signals.meaning: Speed/evasion capability present; tactical maneuver option ready

faction_indicators.faction: Star Fox Team / Corneria Defense Force
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Star Fox emblem (implied through team identity)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corneria planet environment; defense installations; General Pepper's command infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Andross as central figurehead; evil scientist aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy spacecraft types; robotic creatures; large-scale mechanical constructs; Andross as final boss entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Venom planet (evil/hostile aesthetic); industrial/destructive environments; fortress-like structures; organic-mechanical hybrid enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corneria landscape populated with enemy forces, combat activity visible, defensive structures engaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wingmen actively fighting; General Pepper's forces present but overwhelmed; player serves as intervention force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct planetary environments corresponding to easy/normal/hard paths; varying terrain and enemy compositions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density and difficulty scale by chosen route; wingman survival rates vary; different boss encounters per path

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Movement from outer Lylat system planets toward Venom; increasing enemy sophistication; scale of threats escalates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters intensify; wingmen face greater danger; final level stages confrontation with Andross himself

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wingman aircraft disappears from player's view; no longer appears in subsequent levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ally no longer available for support or tactical assistance; player must complete remaining game without that wingman's aid

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arwing wings visually break/detach if clipping too much; visual damage model changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Handling degradation; loss of weapon upgrade capability; reduced maneuverability

environmental_storytelling.example: Corneria depicted as developed, defensible world; Venom depicted as hostile, industrial evil empire stronghold. This binary communicates the good-vs-evil political conflict without explicit narrative exposition.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy force composition as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from small fighter patrols on Corneria to massive mechanical constructs and creatures on Venom visually demonstrates Andross's escalating military threat
Three allies traveling with player communicates that Corneria has military backing and institutional support structure, reinforcing the faction's legitimacy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Terrain hazards as environmental danger worldbuilding
environmental_storytelling.example: Natural and artificial obstacles (gates, structures, terrain) on various planets establish each location as distinct, hazardous space requiring tactical navigation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enters player view fleeing from enemy or being pursued; calls for assistance; will take damage if not aided
Flies alongside player in formation; occasionally engages enemies independently; complains if hit by player fire
Disappears from active gameplay; does not return for remainder of campaign
Pursues player; fires projectiles; attempts to intercept; responds to player evasion
Wingmen absent; large-scale attack patterns; significant health pool; progression gate behavior; unique visual design distinguishing from standard enemies
Handles degraded if wings damaged; reduced speed/maneuverability; visual model reflects structural compromise
Next hit results in craft destruction; increased vulnerability; HUD warning communication

notes: Star Fox uses 3D polygonal visuals as primary communication medium. The game establishes a clear good-vs-evil dichotomy through planetary design (Corneria vs. Venom), faction representation through ally presence, and danger communication through enemy density escalation and wingman distress mechanics. The HUD provides quantitative feedback (shield, ammo, lives) while environmental and behavioral manifestations communicate qualitative world-state. The branching path system uses visual route differentiation to communicate difficulty/challenge level. Structural damage (wing breakage) creates persistent visual consequence for player behavior. Wingman survival mechanics make ally preservation a visible strategic element tied to world-state (if a wingman is downed, the world permanently changes by their absence).
